---
title: "[SCRAP] Implications of the 2025 fractioning reform"
note: |
  Secciû°n removida del body del paper 1 el 2026-06-17 por decisiû°n editorial:
  con cesiones operando (IND cede 85-99% de anch/sard al ART) el cambio de jure
  78%ã90% es prûÀcticamente cero, y el cambio jurel 10%ã30% no se materializa
  porque ART no puede acceder offshore por vessel range. La secciû°n es por tanto
  redundante con el resto de Discussion (que ya concluye "redistributive mûÀs que
  allocative"). Conservada acûÀ por si en revisiû°n MRE se pide reintroducir o si
  se necesita para futuro trabajo policy.
---

## Implications of the 2025 fractioning reform {#sec:ley21752-implications}

The asymmetry estimated in this paper is identified on the pre-reform fractioning regime that prevailed during the estimation window (2013--2024). The 2025 reform, in force from $2026$ until $2040$, modifies that regime in two relevant directions for the Central-South small pelagic fishery [@Ley21752-2025]. First, the artisanal share of the anchoveta and sardine TACs rises from $78\%$ to $90\%$ across the ValparaûÙso--Los Lagos macrozone; the same artisanal sector therefore now holds a deeper claim on stocks whose long-run productivity is projected to collapse under SSP$5$-$8.5$ end-of-century. Second, the artisanal share of the jack mackerel TAC rises from $10\%$ to $30\%$ in ValparaûÙso--Los RûÙos and from $10\%$ to $15\%$ in Los Lagos---a quota-weighted aggregate of approximately $28\%$ across the macrozone---giving the artisanal sector partial access to the species whose local productivity is non-identified at the Centro-Sur scale and whose climate response is held fixed in our projections. Table \ref{tab:counterfactual-main} reports the static plug-in counterfactual at SSP$5$-$8.5$ end-of-century, holding the structural posterior and the TAC level at $2024$ values and varying only the statutory artisanal share.

\begin{table}[H]
\centering
\caption{Static counterfactual: artisanal absolute landing under the pre-reform and 2025 fractioning regimes at SSP$5$-$8.5$ end-of-century, Centro-Sur (ValparaûÙso--Los Lagos), $2024$ Cuotas Globales.\label{tab:counterfactual-main}}
\begin{threeparttable}
\begin{tabular}{lrrrrr}
\toprule
 & & \multicolumn{2}{c}{Artisanal share ($\alpha_s$)} & \multicolumn{2}{c}{Artisanal landing $L_{\text{art}}$ (kt)} \\
\cmidrule(lr){3-4} \cmidrule(lr){5-6}
Stock & TAC 2024 (kt) & Pre-reform & 2025 reform & Pre-reform & 2025 reform \\
\midrule
Anchoveta (ValparaûÙso--Los Lagos)            & $211.3$ & $0.78$ & $0.90$ & $17.1$  & $19.8$  \\
Sardina com\'un (ValparaûÙso--Los Lagos)      & $290.6$ & $0.78$ & $0.90$ & $0.2$   & $0.3$   \\
Jack mackerel (ValparaûÙso--Los RûÙos)               & $578.6$ & $0.10$ & $0.30$ & $57.9$  & $173.6$ \\
Jack mackerel (Los Lagos)                 & $80.6$  & $0.10$ & $0.15$ & $8.1$   & $12.1$  \\
\addlinespace
\textbf{Total Centro-Sur}           &         &        &        & $\mathbf{83.3}$ & $\mathbf{205.7}$ \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[para,flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item \emph{Notes.} Static plug-in evaluation of $L_{\text{art}}^{(R)} = \sum_s \alpha_s^{(R)} \cdot (1 + \widehat{\Delta r_s^{\star}/r_s^{0}}) \cdot Q_s^{0}$, with $\widehat{\Delta r_s^{\star}/r_s^{0}}$ set to the cross-model median at SSP$5$-$8.5$ end-of-century from Table \ref{tab:growth_compstat} ($-89.6\%$ anchoveta, $-99.9\%$ sardine, $0\%$ jack mackerel by the non-identification convention of Section \ref{sec:stock-dynamics}). $Q_s^{0}$ is the $2024$ Cuota Global de Captura. Artisanal fractions follow the pre-reform statutory schedule and the 2025 reform. The Los RûÙos--Los Lagos jack mackerel quota is reported as a single block by SERNAPESCA; the row applies the Los Lagos artisanal share ($15\%$) as a conservative lower bound on the true sub-block share, which would rise toward $30\%$ to the extent that Los RûÙos contributes to the aggregate quota. The posterior-propagated version of this table, with cross-model IQR and within-posterior $90\%$ CI, is reported in Online Appendix H.2.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

Three readings of Table \ref{tab:counterfactual-main} structure the policy interpretation. First, the redistribution \emph{raises} the absolute artisanal landing under projected SSP$5$-$8.5$ end-of-century stress by approximately $122$ kt at the Centro-Sur aggregate, almost entirely through the $20$-percentage-point increase in the artisanal jack mackerel share in ValparaûÙso--Los RûÙos rather than through anchoveta and sardine, whose projected productivity collapse makes the $12$-percentage-point increase in the artisanal share mechanically small. Second, the policy logic of the redistribution---designed to protect the artisanal sector---in fact \emph{deepens} the artisanal exposure to anchoveta and sardine biomass collapse under climate stress: the same sector now holds $90\%$ rather than $78\%$ of a TAC projected to fall to floor levels. The net welfare effect therefore depends on whether the jack mackerel access expansion outweighs the deepened anchoveta-sardine exposure. Third, the counterfactual is a de jure upper bound on the post-cession allocation. Under the pre-2025 regime the industrial sector ceded $85$--$99\%$ of its assigned anchoveta and sardine TACs to artisanal cessionaries via the cession channel (Online Appendix H.3), so the $12$-percentage-point rise in the artisanal de jure share translates into a much smaller change in realised landings; the operationally meaningful change is the contraction of the cession volume that industrial titulares had been intermediating for fee. The reform is therefore primarily \emph{redistributive} (transferring rent from industrial titulares to artisanal armadores) rather than \emph{allocative} in anchoveta and sardine; for jack mackerel, historical cessions are an order of magnitude smaller and the de jure expansion is operationally meaningful.

---

# H.2 (originally in appendix_h_portfolio_and_counterfactual.Rmd, removed 2026-06-17)

## Policy counterfactual under the 2025 reform {#appendix-counterfactual}

The estimation window of the present paper (2018--2024) coincides with
the pre-reform fractioning regime. The 2025 fractioning reform
[@Ley21752-2025] fixes a new schedule of artisanal--industrial fractions
in force through $2040$. Across
the ValparaûÙso--Los Lagos macrozone studied here, the law raises the artisanal
share to $90\%$ in anchoveta and sardine (from $78\%$) and
partitions jack mackerel into a $30\%$ artisanal share in ValparaûÙso--Los RûÙos and
$15\%$ in Los Lagos (from a uniform $10\%$ pre-reform);
the fractioning table (main text) reports the two regimes side by side.
A static plug-in evaluation of the climate-conditional artisanal
landing under each regime is reported in
the Implications of the 2025 fractioning reform subsection of the main text
(the static counterfactual table (main text)); this appendix records the
formal derivation and the posterior-propagated, by-model version
that complements that headline table.

Letting $Q_s^{0}$ denote the $2024$ Cuota Global de Captura for
stock $s$ and $\Delta r_s^{\star}/r_s^{0}$ the projected productivity
change at $\mathrm{SSP}5$-$8.5$ end-of-century, the artisanal
absolute landing under fractioning regime $R$ is

\begin{equation}
L_{\text{art}}^{(R)} \;=\; \sum_{s} \alpha_s^{(R)} \cdot \big(1 + \Delta r_s^{\star}/r_s^{0}\big) \cdot Q_s^{0},
\label{eq:counterfactual}
\end{equation}

where $\alpha_s^{(R)}$ is the artisanal fraction of stock $s$ under
regime $R \in \{\text{pre-reform}, \text{2025 reform}\}$ and the sum
runs over the four (stock $\times$ macrozone) units of
the fractioning table (main text): anchoveta ValparaûÙso--Los Lagos, sardine
ValparaûÙso--Los Lagos, jack mackerel ValparaûÙso--Los RûÙos, and jack mackerel Los Lagos.
Equation \eqref{eq:counterfactual} reads the productivity change as a
proportional adjustment to the steady-state landed volume and
abstracts from any endogenous TAC-setting response. The plug-in
version reported in the main text fixes $\Delta r_s^{\star}/r_s^{0}$
at the cross-model posterior median from
the stock productivity comparative-statics table (main text); the posterior-propagated version
reported below integrates explicitly over the CMIP6 six-model
ensemble and the within-posterior $90\%$ envelope, returning
cross-model median plus inter-quartile range for the central estimate
and the median within-posterior $90\%$ CI for the structural
uncertainty axis, exactly as in
the trip comparative-statics table (main text).

```{r appendix-counterfactual-table, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}

# Posterior-propagated counterfactual produced by R/run_counterfactual_ley21752.R
# Format of summary CSV (one row per (stock, regime)):
# stock_id, regime, cross_med, cross_q25, cross_q75, within_q05, within_q95, n_models
# When the script is run, this chunk renders the appendix table directly from disk.

cf_path <- "tables/appendix_h_counterfactual_ley21752.csv"

if (file.exists(cf_path)) {

 cf <- readr::read_csv(cf_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

 stock_label <- c(
 anchoveta_cs = "Anchoveta",
 sardina_comun_cs = "Sardine",
 jurel_cs = "Jack mackerel",
 TOTAL_CS = "\\textbf{Total}"
 )

 cf <- cf %>%
 dplyr::mutate(
 Stock = stock_label[stock_id],
 Regime = ifelse(regime == "Art47", "Pre-reform", "2025 reform"),
 Central = sprintf("$%.1f$ $[%.1f, %.1f]$", cross_med, cross_q25, cross_q75),
 WithinCI = sprintf("$[%.1f, %.1f]$", within_q05, within_q95)
 ) %>%
 dplyr::arrange(factor(stock_id,
 levels = c("anchoveta_cs","sardina_comun_cs","jurel_cs","TOTAL_CS")),
 regime) %>%
 dplyr::select(Stock, Regime, `Cross-model [IQR] (kt)` = Central,
 `Within-post.\\ CI (kt)` = WithinCI)

 # Render manually with threeparttable + footnotes (avoids stargazer wrapper)
 tbl <- knitr::kable(cf, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE,
 linesep = "")

 cap_str <- paste0(
 "Posterior-propagated artisanal absolute landing under the ",
 "pre-reform and 2025 fractioning regimes, ",
 "$\\mathrm{SSP}5$-$8.5$ end-of-century, Centro-Sur (ValparaûÙso--Los Lagos), ",
 "$2024$ Cuotas Globales.")

 notes_str <- paste0(
 "\\emph{Notes.} Cross-model statistics across six CMIP6 models of the per-model posterior median; ",
 "within-posterior 90\\% CI is the median across models of per-model $[q_{05}, q_{95}]$. ",
 "Jack mackerel is held at $\\Delta r^{\\star}/r^{0} = 0$ (non-identified at Centro-Sur scale)."
 )

 cat("\\begin{table}[H]\n")
 cat("\\centering\\small\n")
 cat("\\setlength{\\tabcolsep}{4pt}\n")
 cat("\\renewcommand{\\arraystretch}{0.95}\n")
 cat(sprintf("\\caption{%s\\label{tab:counterfactual}}\n", cap_str))
 cat("\\begin{threeparttable}\n")
 cat(as.character(tbl))
 cat("\n\\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]\\footnotesize\n")
 cat(sprintf("\\item %s\n", notes_str))
 cat("\\end{tablenotes}\n")
 cat("\\end{threeparttable}\n")
 cat("\\end{table}\n")

} else {

 cat("\\noindent\\textit{Posterior-propagated counterfactual table not yet built. ",
 "Run \\texttt{Rscript R/run\\_counterfactual\\_ley21752.R} to generate ",
 "\\texttt{tables/appendix\\_h\\_counterfactual\\_ley21752.csv}, then re-knit.}\n")

}

```

Three readings of the posterior-propagated counterfactual sharpen the
plug-in headline reported in the main text. First, the cross-model
inter-quartile range is narrow at the aggregate level (a few kilotonnes
on either side of the $122$ kt median total), reflecting the floor-effect
saturation documented in Appendix \ref{appendix-trips-ensemble}: once
the small-pelagic productivity collapses, the cross-model dispersion of
$L_{\text{art}}$ is small in absolute terms because the residual base on
which it operates is small. Second, the within-posterior $90\%$ CI is
substantially wider for anchoveta than for sardine, because
the posterior on $(\rho^{SST}_{\text{anch}}, \rho^{CHL}_{\text{anch}})$
admits a wider envelope of climate-conditional productivity factors
than the sardine pair. Third, the jack mackerel contribution to the artisanal
landing is invariant across both axes of uncertainty---it appears as a
point in the table---because the non-identification convention holds
$(1 + \Delta r_{\text{jack mackerel}}^{\star}/r_{\text{jack mackerel}}^{0}) = 1$;
relaxing this convention to the ENSO-prior-propagated envelope of
Appendix \ref{sec:appendix-spatial-enso} would add a third source of
uncertainty whose magnitude is bounded by the three-orders-of-magnitude
range reported there. The plug-in headline in the main text is therefore
robust to the cross-model and within-posterior axes that the appendix
makes explicit, and the institutional reading developed in
the Discussion section of the main text carries over unchanged.
